employment_information.present_or_last_employer: City of Riverbend – Department of Community Development

education_and_training.major_degree_or_area_of_study: Master of Urban and Regional Planning (MURP), concentration in Community and Economic Development, 2013; B.A. in Sociology, Minor in Environmental Studies, University of Oregon, Eugene, OR, 2008

qualifications_and_interests.board_service_experience:
Riverbend Planning Commission (Vice Chair), City of Riverbend, OR — 2019 to Present
I was appointed to the Riverbend Planning Commission in 2019 and reappointed for a second term in 2023. During my tenure, I have reviewed and made recommendations on comprehensive plan amendments, zoning code updates, and major development proposals, with a focus on ensuring that growth aligns with our community’s adopted values around equity, sustainability, and housing affordability. As Vice Chair since 2022, I help set meeting agendas with staff, coordinate pre-meeting briefings for new commissioners, and occasionally facilitate meetings when the Chair is absent. A major accomplishment was helping shepherd the Riverbend Housing Choices Code Update, which legalized a broader range of housing types (duplexes, triplexes, and cottage clusters) in formerly single-family zones. I worked closely with staff to refine the public engagement strategy, including encouraging the commission to host two listening sessions in Spanish and one youth-focused workshop at the local high school.

Riverbend Bicycle and Pedestrian Advisory Committee (Member), City of Riverbend, OR — 2015 to 2019
Before serving on the Planning Commission, I was a member of the Bicycle and Pedestrian Advisory Committee. In that role, I reviewed and prioritized small-scale active transportation projects, such as crosswalk enhancements, bike lane infill, and sidewalk gap closures. I helped develop the first Safe Routes to School Action Plan for the district, collaborating with school administrators, parents, and the city’s traffic engineer. A notable accomplishment was successfully advocating for the installation of a protected bike lane on Hawthorne Boulevard, which has since seen a measurable increase in bicycle ridership and a reduction in crashes.

Riverbend Community Land Trust (Board Member, Secretary), Nonprofit Organization — 2016 to 2021
I served on the board of the Riverbend Community Land Trust, a nonprofit dedicated to creating permanently affordable homeownership opportunities. As Secretary, I maintained board records, worked on governance policies, and participated in strategic planning retreats. I was part of the team that negotiated our first partnership with a local credit union to offer tailored mortgage products for CLT homebuyers. I also helped draft and adopt the organization’s first Anti-Displacement and Community Engagement Framework, which guided how we prioritized properties and worked with existing residents.

Riverbend Neighborhood Association (At-Large Board Member), 2012 to 2015
Shortly after moving to Riverbend, I joined my neighborhood association board. In that role, I organized neighborhood cleanups, facilitated quarterly community meetings, and served as a liaison between residents and city staff on topics like traffic calming and small business support. A small but meaningful accomplishment was leading a successful effort to convert an underutilized city-owned lot into a community garden, which is still operating and managed by a resident-led committee.
qualifications_and_interests.experience_training_and_skills:
Professional Experience:
I have worked as an urban planner for over a decade, with the past seven years spent as a Senior Urban Planner for the City of Riverbend’s Department of Community Development. In my current role, I lead neighborhood and corridor planning projects, manage interdisciplinary project teams, and facilitate extensive community engagement processes. For example, I recently managed the East Riverbend Corridor Plan, a two-year planning effort that involved over 40 public meetings, three open houses, multiple focus groups with renters and small business owners, and a bilingual survey that received more than 1,200 responses. The resulting plan included a detailed implementation strategy, zoning changes, and capital improvement priorities that were adopted unanimously by the City Council.

Prior to joining the City of Riverbend, I worked as a Planning Associate at Northwest Community Strategies (a small planning and public engagement consulting firm) from 2010 to 2015. There, I supported projects for multiple jurisdictions across Oregon and Washington, including downtown master plans, transit-oriented development studies, and parks and recreation plans. My responsibilities included data analysis, GIS mapping, preparation of staff reports, and facilitation of stakeholder workshops. This consulting background gave me a strong understanding of how different communities approach similar challenges, and how to translate technical planning concepts into accessible language for the public.

Education and Technical Training:
- Master of Urban and Regional Planning (MURP), Portland State University (2013), with a concentration in Community and Economic Development. My capstone project focused on inclusive main street revitalization in lower-income neighborhoods, and involved partnering with a local business association to develop a set of small-business support strategies.
- B.A. in Sociology with a minor in Environmental Studies, University of Oregon (2008). This combination of studies has given me a strong grounding in understanding social dynamics, equity issues, and environmental impacts within the built environment.
- Technical skills: Proficient with GIS (ArcGIS Pro and QGIS), Adobe Creative Suite for visual communication, and data analysis in Excel and basic R. I regularly prepare maps and visualizations that help residents and decision-makers quickly understand complex land use and transportation issues.
- Specialized training: I have completed the Oregon League of Cities’ “Planning Commissioner Training Series” (2020), which covered land use law, public meeting procedures, ethics, and conflict of interest rules. I also completed a 40-hour facilitation and conflict resolution training through the Oregon Mediation Association, which has been particularly useful when navigating contentious public hearings.

Relevant Skills and Experience for Advisory Work:
- Public Engagement and Communication: I have extensive experience designing and facilitating public meetings, workshops, and online engagement tools. I am comfortable presenting technical material in a way that residents with no planning background can understand, and I am intentional about creating space for voices that are often underrepresented, including renters, youth, and non-English speakers. For instance, during the Riverbend Housing Choices Code Update, I worked with local community organizations to co-host small group conversations in apartment complexes, churches, and community centers, rather than relying solely on City Hall meetings.
- Policy Analysis and Writing: A significant part of my job involves analyzing existing policies, identifying gaps or inconsistencies, and drafting new code language, comprehensive plan policies, and staff reports. I am accustomed to reading and interpreting state land use statutes, local development codes, and case law, and then summarizing key points and options for appointed and elected officials.
- Collaborative Problem-Solving: I regularly collaborate with transportation engineers, housing specialists, economic development staff, and environmental planners. I’m comfortable working within a multi-disciplinary environment and balancing multiple policy objectives—such as economic development, environmental sustainability, and social equity—when making recommendations.
- Equity and Inclusion: I identify as a first-generation college graduate from a working-class family, and that background informs my focus on equity in planning. I have participated in the City’s internal racial equity training program and served on the Community Development Department’s Equity Workgroup, where we developed an equity impact review checklist now used for all major planning projects. I am particularly attentive to how zoning and land use decisions can unintentionally reinforce patterns of segregation or displacement.

Language and Community Connections:
I am fluent in English and conversational in Spanish (I can conduct basic outreach conversations and understand community concerns, though I rely on professional interpreters for formal translation). I volunteer with a local nonprofit, Riverbend Welcomes, which supports immigrant and refugee families; this has helped me build relationships with communities that are often underrepresented in formal planning processes.

Overall, my mix of professional planning experience, technical skills, and on-the-ground community work has prepared me to contribute thoughtfully and constructively to an advisory body that deals with complex land use, housing, and community development issues.
